title: cymbalaria glutinosa subsp. brevicalcarata
intro: Cymbalaria glutinosa subsp. brevicalcarata, also known as the 'ivy-leaved toadflax' or 'ivy-leaved toadflax subsp. brevicalcarata', is a sub-species of a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with its leaves and flowers exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
